arguments.task: Research Pumble using official first-party sources only where possible. Find and extract evidence for: product description, whether there is a public API and its type (REST/GraphQL/Both/None), authentication methods for the API, pricing or access gating for API use, any rate limit documentation, and whether there is an official or existing MCP server/integration. Prioritize developer docs, API docs, pricing pages, help/support docs, changelog, GitHub org/repos, and any official docs subdomains. Return exact URLs and concise evidence snippets.

Search the web for official Pumble developer documentation, API documentation, authentication docs, pricing page, support/help docs about apps or API, changelog, GitHub repos, and MCP registry/GitHub entries for Pumble MCP. Prioritize first-party pages and official GitHub/registry pages. Return URLs with concise evidence snippets.

input.thought: Gather official Pumble evidence on API, auth, pricing/access, rate limits, and MCP availability in parallel.

arguments.task: Open this official Pumble Help page and extract exact evidence for API availability, auth method, pricing/access gating, and rate limits. Then open any linked official API reference/docs page and extract whether the API is REST or GraphQL. Return exact URLs and snippets only.

Search official or authoritative sources for Pumble MCP availability. Check official Pumble help/changelog pages, official GitHub org/repos, and reputable MCP registry listings. Determine whether an MCP server exists for Pumble and whether it appears official. Return exact URLs and snippets.

input.thought: Run focused follow-up tasks to verify API details and MCP existence from authoritative sources.

raw_result.description: Team communication app for messaging, file sharing, message history, and video conferencing.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official help page for the API addon says authentication is done with API keys.
raw_result.gating_reason: Official help page states the API addon is available on all plans, indicating self-serve access rather than sales gating.

api_surface.breadth: Public API exists via the Pumble API addon with official API docs hosted at an /api-docs endpoint; evidence gathered supports a documented HTTP API, but endpoint breadth could not be fully verified from the loaded docs.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Search-task evidence indicated a Pumble Help “What’s new” page mentioning an MCP Server, but officiality and implementation details were not fully verified from the page itself in-chat.

raw_result.blocker: API docs page appeared to be a dynamic/blank SPA in browser automation, so endpoint coverage and some implementation details could not be fully verified from the evidence captured here.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official help-page evidence gathered in-browser stated a rate limit of 1000 requests per minute per user.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official help content indicates the API addon is available on all plans; an official API docs URL exists; separate gathered search evidence suggested a help/changelog mention of an MCP Server.
raw_result.human_review_reason: The REST classification and MCP existence are inferred from partial browser-captured evidence. The API docs page did not fully render in-tool, and the MCP mention was not directly opened and verified in-chat. Human review should confirm endpoint format, breadth, and whether the MCP server is official/current.
